title: INTEL MacOS - krunkit - wrong ARM versions in podman-installer-macos-amd64.pkg and universal pkg also has the same issue here
body:
### Issue Description

Both **podman** and **podman-desktop** projects for **x64/Intel Mac** are affected.

Clean install of **5.3.2** from **podman-installer-macos-amd64.pkg** on **Intel** based Macbook Pro with MacOS 15.2 results in WRONG = **ARM64** binary file at /opt/podman/bin/krunkit 

It looks like clear mistake in the release https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.3.2/podman-installer-macos-amd64.pkg and the same issues comes from https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/releases/download/v1.16.0/podman-desktop-1.16.0-x64.dmg and also in the universal packagees for both as well.

Please fix it and provide a proper/**AMD64** version of the /opt/podman/bin/krunkit file in the x86 and universal packages respectively.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Clean install of **5.3.2** from **podman-installer-macos-amd64.pkg** on **Intel** based Macbook Pro with MacOS 15.2
2. run command in terminal = file /opt/podman/bin/krunkit
3. verify the file command output = /opt/podman/bin/krunkit: Mach-O 64-bit executable **arm64**
4. try to run the file = /opt/podman/bin/krunkit
5. get the confirmation of error = -sh: /opt/podman/bin/krunkit: **Bad CPU** type in executable
6. podman info
OS: darwin/amd64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.2
...


### Describe the results you received

/opt/podman/bin/krunkit: Mach-O 64-bit executable **arm64**
/opt/podman/bin/krunkit: **Bad CPU** type in executable


### Describe the results you expected

/opt/podman/bin/krunkit: Mach-O 64-bit executable **x86_64**

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: darwin/amd64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.2
...
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @slp @jakecorrenti we just seem to bundle krunkit of the github releases. Is it expected that you only build for arm64 and no x86_64?

> [@slp](https://github.com/slp) [@jakecorrenti](https://github.com/jakecorrenti) we just seem to bundle krunkit of the github releases. Is it expected that you only build for arm64 and no x86_64?

krunkit (and libkrun) is not supported on Intel Macs, so this is behaving as expected.

Does `podman machine init` return a proper error when trying to use libkrun on x86_64 then?

And I guess for our installer it should not be bundled in the x86_64 installer then.

well, ok, but then the krunkit file should be excluded from x86_64/amd64 installer/packages and also the podman-desktop shouldn't be touching it on IntelMac, while it actually is trying to use it when you have no podman installed beforehand and just clicking via gui to initialize the first/base VM and then it hungs with BAD CPU errors so you have to guess for manually create/initialize the default vm by podman machine init and then the desktop works...

> Does `podman machine init` return a proper error when trying to use libkrun on x86_64 then?
> 
> And I guess for our installer it should not be bundled in the x86_64 installer then.

We have a `SupportedProviders` API but I don't see any checks (at a quick glance) on init to see if the user accidentally selected an unsupported provider and fail gracefully

maybe that's also part of the problem - I've actually selected the GPUenabled/libkrun in the desktop while there was no info that it is unsupported on Intel and it was simply failing to start with just bad CPU error in logs instead of preventing me from choosing the unsupported config.
@k-paz https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25146 should now make Podman return an error if libkrun is used on an Intel machine
@k-paz I'm going to close this issue. If https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25146 didn't fix it, then feel free to re-open the issue
